setup_task.sh and export_result.sh both reference this tag; it is created during task setup and verified in the database checks

pi_items.verified_value: Real US Supreme Court cases from public legal databases, injected into Jurism SQLite database via inject_references.py
pi_items.source: Code analysis of inject_references.py (lines 17-129) containing LEGAL_REFERENCES list with 10 real cases and law review articles from public sources. Field IDs and Item Type IDs verified against Jurism 6.0.30m3 database schema.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses three real US Supreme Court cases: Gideon v. Wainwright (372 U.S. 335, 1963), Miranda v. Arizona (384 U.S. 436, 1966), and Obergefell v. Hodges (576 U.S. 644, 2015). These cases are injected into a Jurism legal reference database from a publicly-available list of real Supreme Court cases and law review articles. A custom collection named 'Liberty & Due Process' is created in the task, and a 'due-process' tag is applied to the target items. The data is not synthetic—all cases are real, documented Supreme Court decisions with correct citations.
